summary: There are significant sex differences in oncogenic mutational processes due to hormone-mediated influences—estrogens promote mutation risk in female cancers like those affecting breasts and ovaries, whereas males display unique mutational profiles driven by androgen-related mechanisms seen in conditions such as testicular cancer. Evidence from both molecular biology and population-level data supports these differential carcinogenesis pathways based on sexual dimorphism in physiology and immunity.
